Catalog Description

Introduction to Computer Systems. (3-2). Credit 4. Introduction to system support

for application programs, both on single node and over network including OS

application interface, inter-process communication, introduction to system and

network programming, and simple computer security concepts; hands-on lab

assignments. Prerequisite: CSCE 221 with a grade of C or better; grade of C or

better in CSCE 312 or concurrent enrollment in CSCE 350 or ECEN 350.

Course Prerequisites

Prerequisite/Corequisite(s):  CSCE 221 with a grade of C or better; grade of C or

better in CSCE 312 or concurrent enrollment in CSCE 350/ECEN 350 or

ECEN 350/CSCE 350.

Course Learning Outcomes

By the end of the semester, students will be able to utilize basic operating-system

services and C/C++ system APIs, create multi-threaded solutions, implement proper

synchronization, and build applications with efficient file I/O and memory

management.

Special Course Designation

None

Grading Policy

Your grade in the class will be based on the following activities spread throughout

the semester.

Labs & Programming Assignments –  50% .

Homeworks –  10%.

In-class graded assignments  –  10%.

Three midterms –  30% . Either no final exam, or final exam in lieu of the third

midterm!

Your letter grade in the class will be based on the following cutoffs where  x  is your

cumulative score.

A: x  ≥  90.

B: 80  ≤  x < 90.

C: 70  ≤  x < 80.

D: 60  ≤  x < 70.

F: x < 60.

Late Work Policy

Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not

considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy ( Student Rule 7 ).

Course Specific Late Work Policy

Late assignments will not be accepted without a reason.

Assignments will usually be due by 11:59 PM on the due date.  To

accommodate last minute submissions, we'll deduct 1% penalty per late

hour on programming assignments . I.e., we'll grade the assignment as if it

was submitted on time, then deduct for late time. In most cases you'll submit

your assignments on Gradescope but if because of some technical hitch we

ask you to mail your assignment to us, you must keep the mail in your

Sent/Outbox folder, so you can later show that you mailed us the assignment

on time. Please discuss unusual circumstances in advance with the TA's when

possible.

Work submitted as makeup work for an excused absence is not considered

late work and is exempted from the late work policy (Student Rule 7).

Email submissions will not be accepted in general. However, you should keep

a copy (in your mail Sent/Outbox folder) of all relevant communication to us

in case of disputes.

If you have difficulty finishing an assignment, contact the instructor/TA before

the deadline.

AI Statement

I'm fine if you use tools such as ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude etc. to understand the

material in the class, and even to help with your homeworks. But  blind copying

and pasting from these tools is not permitted . You must eventually write the

solution yourself, and code your labs and programming assignments yourself. Also

remember that you'll have to take all exams without these tools, and a weaker

understanding resulting from constant use of these tools will reflect in your exam

performance.

Course Specific Attendance Policy

Attendance during lectures is not mandatory . If you are receiving federal

financial assistance, the university uses your “participation” as a measure of meeting

the requirements of the assistance. Physical attendance is one criterion to

determine participation. You should be ok if you're turning in labs and

programming assignments, taking quizzes and exams etc. However, I will take

attendance as an indication of your “participation” and report it to the university

when asked. I may take pictures of the classroom to verify attendance.
